code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test07-MAY-2025Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows mild hypoglycemia and low HbA1c, suggesting possible chronic low glucose or malnutrition. Elevated creatinine may indicate impaired renal function. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance, raising concern for renal impairment and possible dehydration. No evidence of urinary infection. Recommend clinical correlation for renal and metabolic status.
